title: US Public-Sector AI Procurement Clause and Policy Inventory
description: Verified inventory of the clauses, memoranda, rules, and oversight recommendations that govern how US federal and state bodies buy AI: identifiers, status, legal effect, key obligations, deadlines, and primary sources. Every public record was verified against the linked official source.
citation: Vorp Labs, US Public-Sector AI Procurement Clause and Policy Inventory (as of 2026-07-23), https://vorplabs.com/ai-regulatory-updates/us-ai-procurement

records.instrumentTitle: Proposed GSAR clause 552.239-7001, acquisition of information and communication technology
records.keyObligations: LLM and supply-chain disclosure; Government-data and custom-development rights; no training or unrelated use; safeguarding; localization; deletion; incident reporting; portability; change notices; truthfulness and ideological neutrality; Government testing; flowdown.
records.sourceTitle: Federal Register: General Services Acquisition Regulation; Acquisition of Information and Communication Technology
records.notes: A proposal and request for comments, not a final clause. GSA states it is collecting input before possible future deviation or formal rulemaking.

records.instrumentTitle: Proposed flowdown clause 552.239-7001-1 (LLM developer)
records.keyObligations: Flows applicable IP, compliance, privacy, change-notification, and performance requirements to an LLM developer.
records.sourceTitle: Federal Register: General Services Acquisition Regulation; Acquisition of Information and Communication Technology

records.instrumentTitle: Proposed flowdown clause 552.239-7001-2 (LLM system operator)
records.keyObligations: Flows applicable IP, reporting, privacy, portability, change-notification, and performance requirements to an LLM system operator.
records.sourceTitle: Federal Register: General Services Acquisition Regulation; Acquisition of Information and Communication Technology

records.instrumentTitle: Proposed flowdown clause 552.239-7001-3 (LLM system integrator)
records.keyObligations: Flows applicable IP, compliance, privacy, portability, model-version and change-notification, and performance requirements to an LLM system integrator.
records.sourceTitle: Federal Register: General Services Acquisition Regulation; Acquisition of Information and Communication Technology

records.instrumentTitle: Proposed flowdown clause 552.239-7001-4 (LLM service provider)
records.keyObligations: Flows applicable IP, compliance, privacy, portability, change-notification, and performance requirements to an LLM service provider.
records.sourceTitle: Federal Register: General Services Acquisition Regulation; Acquisition of Information and Communication Technology

records.instrumentTitle: M-25-21, Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust
records.keyObligations: Agency AI governance; high-impact-AI determination; pre-deployment testing; AI impact assessments; monitoring; discontinuation or waiver of noncompliant high-impact uses.

records.instrumentTitle: M-24-10, legacy federal AI governance memorandum
records.keyObligations: Rescinded and replaced by M-25-21.

records.instrumentTitle: M-25-22, Driving Efficient Acquisition of Artificial Intelligence in Government
records.keyObligations: Cross-functional acquisition review; American-made AI policy; privacy; IP and data terms; no unauthorized training; portability and lock-in protection; testing; performance monitoring; disclosure analysis; shared internal repository.
records.notes: Applies to covered solicitations and options from September 30, 2025. Agency policy updates were due December 29, 2025. Excludes covered national-security systems.

records.instrumentTitle: M-24-18, legacy responsible-AI-acquisition memorandum
records.keyObligations: Rescinded and replaced by M-25-22.

records.instrumentTitle: M-26-04, Increasing Public Trust in Artificial Intelligence Through Unbiased AI Principles
records.keyObligations: Unbiased AI contractual requirements for LLM procurements; vendor transparency documents; governance, evaluation, provenance, and third-party-control disclosures; relevant terms treated as material to eligibility and payment; user reporting process.
records.notes: The memorandum states its own scheduled sunset of December 11, 2027. This inventory reports the memorandum's requirements as issued and takes no position on them.

records.instrumentTitle: Executive Order 14409, Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security
records.keyObligations: Advanced-AI innovation, cyber defense, system hardening, and protection of US AI intellectual property.
records.notes: No direct acquisition clause mandate was identified in the fetched text. Published at 91 FR 34565 on June 5, 2026.

records.instrumentTitle: National Security Presidential Memorandum 11 (NSPM-11)
records.keyObligations: Anti-disable, anti-degrade, and anti-unapproved-modification clauses or equivalent controls; reliability, robustness, steerability, controllability; testing and interoperability; contract termination and waivers; multi-vendor procurement updates.

records.instrumentTitle: GAO-26-107859 Recommendation 1: systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons learned
records.keyObligations: Update policy to systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons, including contract-clause practices, and submit them to GSA's repository.
records.sourceTitle: GAO-26-107859, Artificial Intelligence Acquisitions: Agencies Should Collect and Apply Lessons Learned to Improve Future Procurements

records.instrumentTitle: GAO-26-107859 Recommendation 2: systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons learned
records.keyObligations: Update policy to systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons, including contract-clause practices, and submit them to GSA's repository.
records.sourceTitle: GAO-26-107859, Artificial Intelligence Acquisitions: Agencies Should Collect and Apply Lessons Learned to Improve Future Procurements

records.instrumentTitle: GAO-26-107859 Recommendation 3: systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons learned
records.keyObligations: Update policy to systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons, including contract-clause practices, and submit them to GSA's repository.
records.sourceTitle: GAO-26-107859, Artificial Intelligence Acquisitions: Agencies Should Collect and Apply Lessons Learned to Improve Future Procurements

records.instrumentTitle: GAO-26-107859 Recommendation 4: systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons learned
records.keyObligations: Update policy to systematically collect AI-acquisition lessons, including contract-clause practices, and submit them to GSA's repository.
records.sourceTitle: GAO-26-107859, Artificial Intelligence Acquisitions: Agencies Should Collect and Apply Lessons Learned to Improve Future Procurements

records.instrumentTitle: GSA CIO 2185.1C, Accelerating Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence at GSA
records.keyObligations: Internal GSA assessment, procurement, approval, monitoring, transparency, lifecycle, risk, privacy, security, IP, interoperability, and American-made-AI governance.

records.instrumentTitle: PIC 25-03A, Artificial Intelligence Acquisition Guidance
records.keyObligations: Contracting officers must include the AI Acquisitions (SEP 2025) requirement statement in all new solicitations, contracts, and orders acquiring covered AI, and add it to extended or amended existing ones. The statement restricts use of NASA data for AI training without written authorization, prohibits training public or commercial models on nonpublic NASA data absent approval, adds vendor lock-in protections and post-contract data and model rights, requires advance notice and OCIO approval for new AI features or model updates, and requires CAIRIA risk-and-impact-assessment reporting with subcontractor flowdown.
records.notes: Initially issued September 30, 2025 and updated February 11, 2026; in effect until the NASA OCIO AI Guide is issued. No NFS clause text changes: obligations enter contracts through PWS, SOW, or SOO requirement statements. A companion AI-use-during-performance disclosure statement is phased in pending NASA parameter definitions.

records.instrumentTitle: PIC 26-02, Artificial Intelligence Large Language Models Acquisition Guidance
records.keyObligations: Contracting officers must include the LLM Transparency Requirements (MAR 2026) statement and LLM Transparency DRD in all new NASA solicitations, contracts, and orders acquiring LLMs or other covered AI models, and add them to extended or amended existing ones. The DRD is due with the proposal and feeds the responsibility determination under FAR 9.104-1; contractors must comply with the Unbiased AI Principles and operate a mechanism to receive and remediate end-user reports. The transparency requirements are material contract terms, and noncompliance is grounds for withholding payment and termination for cause or default.
records.notes: Implements OMB M-26-04; effective as dated and in effect until the NASA OCIO AI Guide is issued. No NFS clause text changes: requirements enter contracts through requirement statements and the LLM Transparency DRD.

records.instrumentTitle: Acquisition Letter 2026-05, Unbiased AI Principles (transmitted by Policy Flash 2026-45)
records.keyObligations: Department-wide procurement policy implementing OMB M-26-04. Requiring activities complete an AI impact assessment before solicitation; solicitations must state the AI use case, performance thresholds, bias testing, model documentation, data provenance, and lifecycle-management requirements. New solicitations, contracts, and modifications acquiring LLMs must incorporate the Ensuring Unbiased AI Principles (APR 2026) clause, whose requirements are material to eligibility and payment, flow down to all tiers, and carry cure, withholding, and termination remedies.
records.sourceTitle: DOE Policy Flash 2026-45, transmitting Acquisition Letter 2026-05
records.notes: The Policy Flash transmits the operative Acquisition Letter PDF (AL 2026-05, May 8, 2026), effective immediately until superseded. Excludes management and operating contracts for now, with separate guidance forthcoming. DOE notes that policy flashes are not themselves statements of policy; the Acquisition Letter is the operative document.

records.instrumentTitle: State Contracting Manual Chapter 23.03, generative AI contract terms
records.keyObligations: All acquisition types must include GenAI terms; current model provisions govern new contracts and specified amendments.

records.instrumentTitle: State Administrative Manual 4986.9, GenAI disclosure and notification language
records.keyObligations: GenAI disclosure and notification language for solicitations and contracts; notification when GenAI is a deliverable or materially affects systems, risk, or performance; pre-award risk assessment.

records.instrumentTitle: Sample generative AI contract provisions
records.keyObligations: Definitions and adaptable terms for judicial-branch agreements; expressly advisory and subject to contract-specific edits.
records.notes: Issued February 2025; current page checked on the tracker date.
